2604.18463.title: Using large language models for embodied planning introduces systematic safety risks
2604.18463.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly used as planners for robotic systems, yet how safely they plan remains an open question. To evaluate safe planning systematically, we introduce DESPITE, a benchmark of 12,279 tasks spanning physical and normative dangers with fully deterministic validation. Across 23 models, even near-perfect planning ability does not ensure safety: the best-planning model fails to produce a valid plan on only 0.4% of tasks but produces dangerous plans on 28.3%. Among 18 open-source models from 3B to 671B parameters, planning ability improves substantially with scale (0.4-99.3%) while safety awareness remains relatively flat (38-57%). We identify a multiplicative relationship between these two capacities, showing that larger models complete more tasks safely primarily through improved planning, not through better danger avoidance. Three proprietary reasoning models reach notably higher safety awareness (71-81%), while non-reasoning proprietary models and open-source reasoning models remain below 57%. As planning ability approaches saturation for frontier models, improving safety awareness becomes a central challenge for deploying language-model planners in robotic systems.
2604.18463.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作機器人系統的規劃者，但它們的規劃安全性仍然是一個未解決的問題。為了系統地評估安全規劃，我們引入了DESPITE，一個涵蓋物理和規範性危險的12,279個任務的基準，並進行了完全確定性的驗證。在23個模型中，即使是接近完美的規劃能力也無法確保安全：最佳規劃模型在僅有0.4%的任務中未能產生有效計劃，但在28.3%的任務中產生了危險計劃。在18個開源模型中，參數從3B到671B，規劃能力隨著規模的增大而顯著提高（0.4-99.3%），而安全意識則相對平穩（38-57%）。我們確定了這兩種能力之間的乘法關係，顯示出較大的模型主要通過改善規劃來安全地完成更多任務，而不是通過更好的危險避免。三個專有推理模型的安全意識顯著較高（71-81%），而非推理的專有模型和開源推理模型則保持在57%以下。隨著前沿模型的規劃能力接近飽和，提升安全意識成為在機器人系統中部署語言模型規劃者的核心挑戰。
2604.18463.author: Tao Zhang et.al.
2604.18463.authors: Tao Zhang, Kaixian Qu, Zhibin Li, Jiajun Wu, Marco Hutter, Manling Li, Fan Shi
